- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- promote: /promote — Promote session work into the Obsidian wiki
- redeploy: Redeploy the homelab by rerunning the most recent boughtmyowncloud GitOps Sync run:
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2	name: Rockybot Architecture Reference
3	description: Full codebase map for rockybot — services, data model, watchers, Quartz/notes-web, key file paths. Read this at session start instead of re-exploring.
4	type: reference

6	---
7	## Rockybot — Architecture Reference
8	
9	**Repo:** `/home/ubuntu/src/rockybot`  
10	**Version (as of last update):** 1.0.1 → next: 1.1.0 (unreleased on dev)
11	
12	---
13	
14	### Top-Level Structure
15	
16	```
17	/services/bot/            Main polling bot + all 5 watchers
18	/services/obsidian-bridge/  Vault file watcher → Quartz static site builder
19	/services/obsidian-headless/  Obsidian Sync daemon (remote deployments only)
20	/vault-scaffold/          Default vault structure + prompt templates (baked into bot image)
21	/docs/                    User-facing documentation
22	/tests/                   Unit + integration tests
23	/scripts/release.sh       Semver bump + CHANGELOG + git tag automation
24	docker-compose.yml        Production 4-service stack
25	docker-compose.dev.yml    Dev stack (single container, temp vault, tight budgets)
26	.env.example              All env vars with defaults
27	```
28	
29	---
30	
31	### Data Model
32	
33	**Storage:** Markdown files with YAML frontmatter — no database. Vault directory is the single source of truth.
34	
35	**Standard frontmatter fields:**
36	```yaml
37	tags: [research-request]
38	created: 2026-04-17
39	status: pending|processing|completed|error|awaiting-input
40	updated: 2026-04-17
41	sources:
42	  - url: https://...
43	    title: "Source Title"
44	related: ["[[wikilink]]", "[[other]]"]
45	publish: true|false   # Quartz publication gate
46	```
47	
48	**Research topic layout:**
49	```
50	research/<topic>/
51	├── index.md            Overview, TL;DR, sub-page list (publish: true here gates Quartz)
52	├── options-comparison.md
53	├── tradeoffs.md
54	└── ...other sub-pages
55	```
56	
57	**Note-to-note links:** Obsidian wikilink format `[[research/topic/page|display text]]` — file-path relative to vault root. No separate ID system.
58	
59	**Callout syntax (inline task triggers):**
60	```
61	> [!claude]    → amend-watcher: targeted inline edit
62	> [!expand]    → expand-watcher: create new sub-page
63	> [!revise]    → revise-watcher: corpus-wide rewrite of topic
64	```
65	
66	---
67	
68	### Services / Docker Stack
69	
70	| Service | Image | Role | Port |
71	|---|---|---|---|
72	| `rockybot` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot` | Main polling bot, all Claude tasks | internal |
73	| `rockybot-bridge` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge` | Vault watcher → Quartz build | internal |
74	| `notes-web` | `nginx:alpine` | Serves built Quartz static site | 8080 |
75	| `rockybot-headless` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless` | Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) | internal |
76	
77	**Shared volumes:**
78	- `./data/vault:/vault` — vault (shared between bot, bridge, headless)
79	- `./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output` — built site (shared between bridge, notes-web)
80	
81	---
82	
83	### Bot Watchers
84	
85	All watchers serialized by global mutex in `index.js`. Serial execution order: inbox → amend → expand → revise → lint.
86	
87	| Watcher | File | Trigger | Budget | Model |
88	|---|---|---|---|---|
89	| **inbox** | `inbox-watcher.js` | `research/inbox/*.md` with `status: pending` | $2.00 | sonnet |
90	| **amend** | `amend-watcher.js` | `> [!claude]` callout anywhere | $0.50 | haiku |
91	| **expand** | `expand-watcher.js` | `> [!expand]` callout | $1.00 | sonnet |
92	| **revise** | `revise-watcher.js` | `> [!revise]` in topic `index.md` | $4.00 | sonnet |
93	| **lint** | `lint-watcher.js` | `status: pending` in `research/lint-trigger.md` | $5.00 | sonnet |
94	
95	Inbox watcher runs a **Karpathy ingest pass** as its final step: Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages.
96	
97	---
98	
99	### Claude Integration
100	
101	**No REST API.** Claude invoked as subprocess via `claude --print <prompt>`.
102	
103	- **Runner:** `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — spawns CLI, collects stdout/stderr, never rejects
104	- **Billing:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (default) or `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` (subscription first, API fallback)
105	- **Interactive auth:** If subscription fails and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts OAuth URL to Discord as button; 5-min timeout before fallback
106	- **Budget:** `--max-budget-usd` flag (API-key mode only; subscription mode ignores budget flag)
107	- **Prompts:** Loaded from vault `research/*-prompt.md`; user-editable in Obsidian; hardcoded fallbacks if missing
108	
109	---
110	
111	### Topic Export
112	
113	Published topics can be downloaded as self-contained HTML ZIPs from the notes-web UI.
114	
115	**Flow:** Browser clicks "⬇ Export ZIP" on root index → `GET /export/<slug>` → nginx proxies to bridge:3001 → bridge validates, generates ZIP in memory, streams download.
116	
117	**Security gates (in order):**
118	1. Slug format: must match `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` — blocks path traversal before any filesystem access
119	2. Publish whitelist: `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` called fresh on every request (never cached); slug must be in published list
120	3. Quartz output must exist (`quartzOutput/<slug>/` dir)
121	4. Concurrency guard: 429 if already building same topic
122	
123	**New files:**
124	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation: walks topic HTML, rewrites paths via cheerio (static assets → relative, within-topic links → relative, cross-topic links → neutered with tooltip), includes Quartz `static/` dir
125	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node `http` server on port 3001 with all security gates; calls `buildTopicExport` on valid requests
126	
127	**New dependencies:** `archiver@^7`, `cheerio@^1` in `obsidian-bridge/package.json`
128	
129	**nginx:** `/export/` location proxies to `http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/`; bridge exposes port 3001 internally (`expose: ["3001"]` in docker-compose)
130	
131	---
132	
133	### Notes-Web / Quartz Frontend
134	
135	**Not a dynamic web app.** Pre-rendered static HTML by Quartz v4.4.0.
136	
137	- **Bridge** (`services/obsidian-bridge/src/`) watches vault with chokidar (5s poll, 3s write-debounce), runs Quartz build on change
138	- **Publish gate:** Only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in build
139	- **Link rendering:** Wikilinks `[[topic/page]]` → relative HTML links `<a href="/topic/page/">`
140	- **Features:** Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults)
141	- **Explorer navigation:** Bridge startup patches `quartz.layout.ts` at runtime to set `folderClickBehavior: "link"` so clicking a topic folder navigates to its index page (not just toggle)
142	- **nginx config:** `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`, serves `/quartz-output/` on port 8080, 7-day cache on static assets; rewrites `/research/*` → `/*` to fix vault-absolute wikilinks; proxies `/export/*` → bridge port 3001
143	
144	---
145	
146	### Key File Paths
147	
148	**Bot core:**
149	- `services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, global mutex, watcher orchestration
150	- `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess + auth fallback
151	- `services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord/Twilio notifications
152	- `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot (interactive auth)
153	- `services/bot/src/login-runner.js` — `claude login` OAuth URL capture
154	
155	**Watchers:**
156	- `services/bot/src/inbox-watcher.js`
157	- `services/bot/src/amend-watcher.js`
158	- `services/bot/src/expand-watcher.js`
159	- `services/bot/src/revise-watcher.js`
160	- `services/bot/src/lint-watcher.js`
161	
162	**Bridge:**
163	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` — chokidar watcher setup, runtime quartz.layout.ts patch
164	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` — Quartz build + publish gate (`getPublishedTopics`)
165	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting via cheerio
166	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node http server on :3001 with all security gates
167	- `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` — static site + /research/ redirect + /export/ proxy
168	
169	**Tests:**
170	- `Dockerfile.test` / `docker-compose.test.yml` — pre-baked test image (node:20-slim + unzip + npm ci); run with `npm run test:integration:docker`
171	- `tests/integration/quartz-builder.test.js` — getPublishedTopics
172	- `tests/integration/export-builder.test.js` — rewriteHtml + buildTopicExport ZIP structure
173	- `tests/integration/http-server.test.js` — HTTP security gates (49 tests total, no Claude needed)
174	
175	**Vault scaffold (user-editable prompts):**
176	- `vault-scaffold/research/research-prompt.md`
177	- `vault-scaffold/research/amend-prompt.md`
178	- `vault-scaffold/research/expand-prompt.md`
179	- `vault-scaffold/research/revise-prompt.md`
180	- `vault-scaffold/research/lint-prompt.md`
181	
182	**Docs (human-facing):**
183	- `docs/INSTALLATION.md`, `docs/WORKFLOW.md`, `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`, `docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md`, `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`
184	- `CHANGELOG.md`
185	
186	---
187	
188	### Release Process
189	
190	1. `./scripts/release.sh` on `main` — shows commits since last tag, proposes semver bump
191	2. User confirms → script updates `services/bot/package.json` version + `CHANGELOG.md`, creates git tag
192	3. Push tag → GitHub Actions builds & pushes images to GHCR
193	4. Image tags: `latest`, `vX.Y.Z`, `vX.Y`
194	5. `dev` branch → `edge`/`dev` images (pre-release)
195	
196	**Conventional commits:** `feat:` → minor, `fix:` → patch, `feat!:`/`fix!:` → major, `docs:`/`chore:`/`test:` → no bump
197	
198	---
199	
200	### Key Design Decisions
201	
202	- **No database:** Vault files are source of truth; direct Obsidian access
203	- **Global mutex:** Prevents concurrent Claude runs + vault conflicts
204	- **File-based state:** Status in frontmatter survives restarts/crashes
205	- **Prompts in vault:** User-customizable without redeploying
206	- **Subscription + API fallback:** Layer budget strategies
207	- **Quartz static site:** Zero-cost hosting; opt-in publish flag per topic
208	- **Obsidian Sync (not LiveSync):** LiveSync conflict resolution breaks status frontmatter fields
209

---

**Storage:** Markdown files with YAML frontmatter — no database. Vault directory is the single source of truth.

**Standard frontmatter fields:**
```yaml

status: pending|processing|completed|error|awaiting-input

```

```
├── index.md            Overview, TL;DR, sub-page list (publish: true here gates Quartz)
├── options-comparison.md

```

**Callout syntax (inline task triggers):**
```

> [!expand]    → expand-watcher: create new sub-page
> [!revise]    → revise-watcher: corpus-wide rewrite of topic
```

---

| Service | Image | Role | Port |
|---|---|---|---|
| `rockybot` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot` | Main polling bot, all Claude tasks | internal |
| `rockybot-bridge` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge` | Vault watcher → Quartz build | internal |

| `rockybot-headless` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless` | Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) | internal |

**Shared volumes:**
- `./data/vault:/vault` — vault (shared between bot, bridge, headless)
- `./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output` — built site (shared between bridge, notes-web)

---

### Bot Watchers

All watchers serialized by global mutex in `index.js`. Serial execution order: inbox → amend → expand → revise → lint.

| Watcher | File | Trigger | Budget | Model |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **inbox** | `inbox-watcher.js` | `research/inbox/*.md` with `status: pending` | $2.00 | sonnet |
| **amend** | `amend-watcher.js` | `> [!claude]` callout anywhere | $0.50 | haiku |
| **expand** | `expand-watcher.js` | `> [!expand]` callout | $1.00 | sonnet |
| **revise** | `revise-watcher.js` | `> [!revise]` in topic `index.md` | $4.00 | sonnet |
| **lint** | `lint-watcher.js` | `status: pending` in `research/lint-trigger.md` | $5.00 | sonnet |

Inbox watcher runs a **Karpathy ingest pass** as its final step: Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages.

---

### Claude Integration

**No REST API.** Claude invoked as subprocess via `claude --print <prompt>`.

- **Runner:** `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — spawns CLI, collects stdout/stderr, never rejects
- **Billing:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (default) or `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` (subscription first, API fallback)
- **Interactive auth:** If subscription fails and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts OAuth URL to Discord as button; 5-min timeout before fallback
- **Budget:** `--max-budget-usd` flag (API-key mode only; subscription mode ignores budget flag)
- **Prompts:** Loaded from vault `research/*-prompt.md`; user-editable in Obsidian; hardcoded fallbacks if missing

---

### Topic Export

Published topics can be downloaded as self-contained HTML ZIPs from the notes-web UI.

**Flow:** Browser clicks "⬇ Export ZIP" on root index → `GET /export/<slug>` → nginx proxies to bridge:3001 → bridge validates, generates ZIP in memory, streams download.

**Security gates (in order):**
1. Slug format: must match `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` — blocks path traversal before any filesystem access
2. Publish whitelist: `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` called fresh on every request (never cached); slug must be in published list
3. Quartz output must exist (`quartzOutput/<slug>/` dir)
4. Concurrency guard: 429 if already building same topic

**New files:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation: walks topic HTML, rewrites paths via cheerio (static assets → relative, within-topic links → relative, cross-topic links → neutered with tooltip), includes Quartz `static/` dir
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node `http` server on port 3001 with all security gates; calls `buildTopicExport` on valid requests

**New dependencies:** `archiver@^7`, `cheerio@^1` in `obsidian-bridge/package.json`

**nginx:** `/export/` location proxies to `http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/`; bridge exposes port 3001 internally (`expose: ["3001"]` in docker-compose)

---

### Notes-Web / Quartz Frontend

**Not a dynamic web app.** Pre-rendered static HTML by Quartz v4.4.0.

- **Bridge** (`services/obsidian-bridge/src/`) watches vault with chokidar (5s poll, 3s write-debounce), runs Quartz build on change
- **Publish gate:** Only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in build
- **Link rendering:** Wikilinks `[[topic/page]]` → relative HTML links `<a href="/topic/page/">`
- **Features:** Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults)
- **Explorer navigation:** Bridge startup patches `quartz.layout.ts` at runtime to set `folderClickBehavior: "link"` so clicking a topic folder navigates to its index page (not just toggle)
- **nginx config:** `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`, serves `/quartz-output/` on port 8080, 7-day cache on static assets; rewrites `/research/*` → `/*` to fix vault-absolute wikilinks; proxies `/export/*` → bridge port 3001

---

### Key File Paths

**Bot core:**
- `services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, global mutex, watcher orchestration
- `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess + auth fallback
- `services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord/Twilio notifications
- `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot (interactive auth)

**Watchers:**

- `services/bot/src/expand-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/revise-watcher.js`

**Bridge:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` — chokidar watcher setup, runtime quartz.layout.ts patch
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` — Quartz build + publish gate (`getPublishedTopics`)
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting via cheerio
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node http server on :3001 with all security gates
- `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` — static site + /research/ redirect + /export/ proxy

**Tests:**
- `Dockerfile.test` / `docker-compose.test.yml` — pre-baked test image (node:20-slim + unzip + npm ci); run with `npm run test:integration:docker`
- `tests/integration/quartz-builder.test.js` — getPublishedTopics

**Vault scaffold (user-editable prompts):**

- `vault-scaffold/research/expand-prompt.md`

**Docs (human-facing):**
- `docs/INSTALLATION.md`, `docs/WORKFLOW.md`, `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`, `docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md`, `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

---

### Release Process

1. `./scripts/release.sh` on `main` — shows commits since last tag, proposes semver bump
2. User confirms → script updates `services/bot/package.json` version + `CHANGELOG.md`, creates git tag
3. Push tag → GitHub Actions builds & pushes images to GHCR
4. Image tags: `latest`, `vX.Y.Z`, `vX.Y`
5. `dev` branch → `edge`/`dev` images (pre-release)

**Conventional commits:** `feat:` → minor, `fix:` → patch, `feat!:`/`fix!:` → major, `docs:`/`chore:`/`test:` → no bump

---

### Key Design Decisions

- **No database:** Vault files are source of truth; direct Obsidian access

content.text: Good, I have solid context. Let me also quickly look at the current claude-runner.js to understand exactly how the fallback is currently implemented.
Now I have a complete picture. Let me also check notifier.js briefly.

I can see exactly what happened. The rate-limit branch at line 116-118 of `claude-runner.js` just silently falls back — no Discord notification, no user decision. The auth failure path has the interactive flow; the rate-limit path has nothing.

Here's what I need to understand before designing the solution:

---

**1. Hold vs requeue when waiting for reset**

When the rate limit hits mid-queue, the global mutex is held. Two options:
- **Hold the mutex** — bot blocks entirely while waiting. Simple, nothing else processes.
- **Requeue** — mark the current file back to `status: pending`, release the mutex, let the normal poll cycle pick it up when the limit resets.

Which do you prefer? Holding is simpler but freezes the queue. Requeue is cleaner but the task gets re-run from scratch (not from mid-way).

**2. What does "pick up from where it left off" mean to you?**

If a task was mid-execution (e.g., Claude had already written 2 sub-pages before hitting the limit), do you want it to retry the *whole* task from scratch on resume, or resume from some checkpoint? The bot doesn't have checkpointing today — it re-runs the entire watcher prompt each time. Is restart-from-scratch acceptable?

**3. Non-interactive case (webhook-only, no Discord WebSocket bot)**

Your `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH` env var gates whether the Discord bot sends interactive buttons vs just a webhook embed. If you're in webhook-only mode (no buttons possible), what should the rate-limit behavior be?
- Notify via webhook + hold indefinitely until limit resets (then auto-retry)?
- Notify via webhook + fall back to API key (current behavior)?

**4. True indefinite or a safety ceiling?**

You said "no timeout" — but if the limit somehow never resets (misconfiguration, account issue), should there be any upper bound (e.g., 24 hours) that triggers a fallback/alert? Or truly indefinite?

**5. How often to poll for reset while waiting?**

When the bot is parked waiting for the subscription limit to reset, how often should it probe Claude to check if it's cleared? Every 15 minutes? Every hour? The Claude Pro limit resets every 5 hours, so the polling interval determines how fast it detects recovery.

Good — interactive mode IS on in the deployed config (`DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true` is the default at docker-compose line 530). So the Discord bot with buttons is active.

On point 5 — you're right that Claude Code's usage limit error includes a reset time. The exact stderr format is something like `"Claude AI usage limit reached. Resets at 2:00 PM"`. We can parse that and sleep until then rather than polling blindly.

Here's the full plan:

---

## Root cause

In `claude-runner.js:116-118`, the `rate-limit` branch does this:

```js
} else if (errorType === 'rate-limit') {
  logger.warn('claude-runner: subscription rate-limit hit — falling back to API key');
}
// falls through to API key with zero Discord notification
```

The auth failure path has the full interactive Discord flow. The rate-limit path has nothing.

---

## Plan

### 1. Parse reset time from stderr

Add a helper that grabs the reset time out of Claude's error message (e.g., `"Resets at 2:00 PM"` → a `Date` object). If no time is found, default to polling every 30 minutes.

### 2. New `askRateLimitDecision()` in `discord-bot.js`

Mirrors `askAuthDecision` but simpler — two buttons, no OAuth URL, 24h timeout instead of 5 min:

- **"⏳ Wait for reset"** — bot holds mutex, retries when limit clears
- **"💰 Use API Key"** — falls back immediately (charges apply)

Message includes the parsed reset time if available: `"Limit resets at 2:00 PM — holding until then."`

### 3. New `handleRateLimitFailure()` in `claude-runner.js`

Called instead of the silent fallback when `errorType === 'rate-limit'` in subscription mode:

1. Parse reset time from stderr
2. If `discordBot.isEnabled()`: call `askRateLimitDecision()`, wait for button press
3. If webhook-only (non-interactive): post a webhook notification + auto-wait (no user choice, just holds)
4. If decision is `'wait-for-reset'`:
   - Sleep until parsed reset time (or 30-min poll if no time)
   - Retry subscription call
   - If still rate-limited: keep waiting
   - 24h hard cap — after 24h, fall back to API key + post a "giving up, using API key" Discord notification
5. If decision is `'use-api-key'`: fall through to existing API key path

### 4. Wire up in `runClaude()`

Replace the silent `logger.warn` + fall-through with a call to `handleRateLimitFailure()`. If it returns `'use-api-key'`, proceed to the existing fallback. If it returns normally (wait succeeded), `return` — task is done.

---

**One thing I'll need to verify before coding:** the exact format of Claude CLI's rate limit stderr so the regex is right. I can look at the actual patterns or do a test parse against the log line from last night if you have it. Do you have any of the container logs from when it hit the limit? content.text:
Found it. There are two bugs, not one:

**1. Wrong classification.** The CLI outputs `"You've hit your limit · resets 7am (UTC)"` on stderr. But `"hit your limit"` isn't in `RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS` — only `"usage limit"` is. So `classifyStderr` returns `'other'` or falls into the empty-stderr path, which routes it to the **auth** handler instead.

**2. Auth handler can't help.** `handleAuthFailure` tries to run `claude login` to get an OAuth URL — but `claude login` exits cleanly when you're just rate-limited (not actually deauthed), so it prints no OAuth URL and the whole thing falls back to API key silently.

The reset time format is also clear: `"resets 7am (UTC)"` — parseable into a target wake time.

---

So the fix needs:
1. Add `"hit your limit"` (and `"you've hit"`) to `RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS`
2. Add a `parseResetTime(stderr)` helper that extracts `"7am (UTC)"` → a `Date`
3. Add `askRateLimitDecision(label, resetTime)` to `discord-bot.js` — two buttons, 24h timeout
4. Add `handleRateLimitFailure(label, stderr)` to `claude-runner.js` — posts Discord, holds mutex, sleeps until reset time, retries subscription, 24h hard cap
5. Wire up in `runClaude()` to call `handleRateLimitFailure` instead of the silent fallback

content.text: Found it. The bug is that `fakeProc()` calls `setImmediate` immediately when the proc is *created* (inside `mockReturnValueOnce`), not when `spawn()` is called. For tests with a sleep in between spawns, proc2's `setImmediate` fires before its listener is attached. The fix: use `mockImplementationOnce(() => fakeProc(...))` so each proc is created on-demand when `spawn()` is actually called.

content.text:
Pushed to `dev`. The edge image build will kick off shortly. Once it's done, redeploy the homelab to pick up the new `edge` image.

Here's a summary of what was fixed and why it took some detective work:

**Root cause (two bugs):**
1. `"You've hit your limit"` was on Claude's **stdout**, not stderr — so `classifyOutput` (formerly `classifyStderr`) never matched it. It fell through to the auth handler, which tried `claude login` on a usage-limit error (useless), then silently used the API key.
2. Even if it had been classified correctly, the old rate-limit path had no Discord notification at all.

**What changes now:**
- Rate-limit hit → Discord posts a message with two buttons: **⏳ Wait for reset** / **💰 Use API Key**
- Reset time is parsed from the error message (`"resets 7am (UTC)"`) so the bot sleeps until the exact moment, not on a 30-min polling loop
- If you click **Wait**: bot holds the mutex, sleeps until reset + 2 min buffer, retries subscription automatically
- If you click **Use API Key** (or wait 24h with no response): falls back to paid key with another notification
- Webhook-only mode: posts a notification embed and auto-waits (same hold behavior, no button required)
